- hash=cddd7d2c6a6ceea0 appears at top (7,25) and bottom pink (54,45)
Let me map out all the top grid symbol hashes and see which ones appear in the bottom bars. This will help me understand if the goal is to make bottom symbols match top symbols.

sections.content:
World model:
- **RIGHT/LEFT:** Move both markers horizontally (7 cols per position)
- **UP/DOWN:** Cycle symbol at selected position in pink bar (reversible)
- **DOWN reversed UP** (hash 4d73→c66a), confirming bidirectional cycling
- **Markers at position 2** (cols 22-26)
- **Goal hypothesis:** Match bottom bar symbols to top grid symbols
- Budget: ~54 target actions, used 3
Plan: First go LEFT to return to start, then systematically check which top symbols match bottom bar symbols to understand the goal.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Go back to leftmost position
action(['LEFT'])

# Now map out all symbol hashes in top grid and bottom bars
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

# Top grid symbols (black objects in top section)
top_symbols = []
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'B':
        center_row = sum(p[0] for p in n['boundary']) / len(n['boundary'])
        if center_row < 33:
            center_col = sum(p[1] for p in n['boundary']) / len(n['boundary'])
            top_symbols.append((center_row, center_col, n['hash'], n['pixels']))

# Bottom bar symbols
bottom_symbols = []
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'B':
        center_row = sum(p[0] for p in n['boundary']) / len(n['boundary'])
        if center_row >= 33:
            center_col = sum(p[1] for p in n['boundary']) / len(n['boundary'])
            bottom_symbols.append((center_row, center_col, n['hash'], n['pixels']))

print("Top grid symbols (3x4):")
top_by_row = {}
for r, c, h, p in sorted(top_symbols):
    row_key = int(r // 9)  # approximate row grouping
    top_by_row.setdefault(row_key, []).append((c, h, p))
    
# Better grouping by actual row
top_sorted = sorted(top_symbols, key=lambda x: (int(x[0]//9)*10, x[1]))
for i, (r, c, h, p) in enumerate(top_sorted):
    col = i % 4
    row = i // 4
    print(f"  [{row},{col}] center=({r:.0f},{c:.0f}) hash={h} pixels={p}")

print("\nBottom bar symbols:")
for r, c, h, p in sorted(bottom_symbols, key=lambda x: (x[0], x[1])):
    bar = "sky_blue" if r < 50 else "pink"
    pos = int((c - 15) / 7)
    print(f"  [{bar},pos{pos}] center=({r:.0f},{c:.0f}) hash={h} pixels={p}")

# Check which top hashes appear in bottom
top_hashes = set(h for _, _, h, _ in top_symbols)
bottom_hashes = set(h for _, _, h, _ in bottom_symbols)
print(f"\nTop hashes in bottom: {top_hashes & bottom_hashes}")
print(f"Bottom hashes NOT in top: {bottom_hashes - top_hashes}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
